register.note: Full-bleed contour landscape (now spanning content width, per-axis window keeps it undistorted) with one perpendicular, slope-stretched gradient arrow and a STRAIGHT-UPHILL caption — a composed poster legible as a thumbnail.
Draws the mechanism itself — perpendicular-to-contours and length-scales-with-steepness — the way Seeing-Theory draws the squared residuals, not just the data.
The canvas self-labels (kicker, live grad/slope/height readouts) and prose terms are tinted to the grammar like Distill's labelled small-multiples.
Warm restrained surface, clean one-peak/one-arrow focal hierarchy; the grid now scales with size so the contour bands are crisp rather than stair-stepped at full width.
Explanatory controls docked to the canvas (draggable point, ascent/descent toggle, live readouts) plus a sticky scroll-spine clear the floor at 3 under the cluster's affordance standard.
Grammar holds — blue gradient arrow, neutral earth terrain, teal live controls, no all-red error wash, prose hues paired with weight+underline.

hero.note: Now full-bleed: the landscape fills the content width (per-axis window keeps pixels square so contours stay round and the arrow truly perpendicular), with the callout below; grid scales with size for crisp contours.

assessment.note: The closing transfer (transfer-training-plateau) is now an open write-your-own prompt; the experiment is a hands-on draggable point.

verdict.summary: Full-bleed hero clears hero-as-protagonist and mechanism at floor 3 with crisp contours; every register dimension clears floor 3.
